draft.introduction: Arjuna is granted divine vision to see Krishna’s cosmic form (viśvarūpa)—an infinite body containing all gods, beings, and worlds simultaneously. The vision terrifies as much as it illumines.

translation.text: Sanjaya said: Having spoken thus, the great Lord of Yoga showed Arjuna his supreme divine form—with many mouths and eyes, many wondrous sights, many divine ornaments, many divine weapons uplifted; wearing divine garlands and garments, all-wonderful, resplendent, infinite, facing every direction.
translation.original_text: evam uktvā tato rājan mahā-yogeśvaro hariḥ | darśayām āsa pārthāya paramaṃ rūpam aiśvaram

translation.text: The Supreme Lord said: I am Time, the great destroyer of worlds, and I have come here to engage all people. Even without your participation, all the warriors standing arrayed in the opposing armies shall cease to exist.
translation.original_text: kālo ’smi loka-kṣaya-kṛt pravṛddho lokān samāhartum iha pravṛttaḥ

translator_notes.note: Oppenheimer famously quoted this verse upon witnessing the first nuclear detonation. The original context is not destruction for its own sake but the revelation that time itself is the divine agent of cosmic dissolution.
